message: Command audit: review all / commands for relevance — do we still need all of them?
Multi-agent handoff: each agent needs {agent}-handoff.md. Handoff tool hardcoded to handoff.md — must support per-agent files. workstream-create must scaffold these.
Worktree merge-main tool: agents need pre-approved git merge main to self-update from main checkout. Currently manual.
Review mdpal bootstrap transcripts to learn what worked/didn't in agent bootstrap (self-orient, read handoffs, follow Next Action)
Flag tool persistence: flags must survive session boundaries — git add on write, warn on SessionStart if unprocessed
Bug: agent-create does not register the agent in settings.json permissions
Bug: Ghostty tab-status hook icons wrong — filled circle for idle (should be hollow), filled triangle for input-needed (should be triangle with !)
Bug: --name flag on claude CLI does not set AGENTNAME env var — tabs all show 'agent' instead of actual agent name
Bug: /feedback command is broken — gives feedback SHA but feedback is never actually filed. Silent failure.
Bug: agency-init blocked on non-main branch (HEAD) — fresh git init repos have no branch yet
Bug: BATS tests writing to live repo INDEX.md and releases.md instead of test fixtures — recurring test isolation failure
Bug: agency-init wrote flat principals format (jordan: jordan) instead of nested (jdm: { name: jordan }) — system username vs principal name confusion
Friction: bootstrap agents ask permission to read their own handoff, role definition, and workstream knowledge files — need pre-approved in settings.json
Friction: brace expansion in bash mkdir commands triggers permission prompts — agency-init must avoid these patterns
Friction: bootstrap agents need prompting before they act — 'act on startup' in handoff not working as auto-start
Friction: stop hook fires repeatedly about uncommitted changes but agent fails to commit — same file flagged 3+ consecutive times
Friction: hookify rule messages are full-screen — should be one-liners with doc pointer
Process: handoffs should have typed frontmatter (session-restore, agency-bootstrap, agent-bootstrap) so tools distinguish handoff purpose
Process: dispatches sent to old path claude/principals/ — monofolk still using pre-migration layout
Tool gap: need transcript review/mining automation — manually mining 58MB transcripts is expensive
Tool gap: need environment-setup skill that detects platform, guides provider setup, does what it can while guiding humans on what only they can do
Tool gap: dispatch tool should auto-mark dispatches as read
Tool gap: need test management — multiple frameworks, need to define what a run for a given boundary looks like
Decision: drop iTerm support, go Ghostty-only for terminal status integration
Decision: kill agency-service and all old services — replaced by ISCP + dispatches model
Decision: the-agency-starter repo is sunset — agency-init replaces it
Decision: kill /agency dispatcher skill — old model, skills replace it
Decision: agency init runs BEFORE claude init (git init -> agency init -> claude init)
Decision: seeds location — should seeds live in workstream dir or usr/{principal}/{agent}/?
Manual: launching worktree agents required giving user CLI commands to copy-paste — should be automated
Manual: session transcript extraction required custom inline scripts every time — should be a tool
Convention: worktree naming in status line redundant — name shown 3 times
Convention: Explore agent on bootstrap made 22 tool calls (/bin/zsh.37) for lightweight orientation — overkill
Convention: inline script without why-comment and without saving to tools/ — the pattern that motivated the discipline rule
Capture why you wrote that script at the top and add it to your tools/ directory.
Transcripts must get to master ASAP and be accessible to any workstream. Formalize transcript location and commit discipline.
agent-create does not create bootstrap handoff — skill says it's the single write path but it doesn't write one
No tech-lead template in agent-create — primary agent class has no template, only generic/docs/tester/etc
agent-create generates placeholder agent.md with [Key responsibility] and wrong myclaude command
agent-create creates extra files (IDEAS.md, ONBOARDING.md, logs/permissions.md) not specified in workstream-create
All non-destructive tools (flag, dispatch read/list/fetch, transcript) must have pre-approved permissions in settings.json — zero prompts for read/write operations on agent's own files
Adopt the vouch model into the-agency. Look at Ghostty's CONTRIBUTING and AI-POLICY as starting points for our own.
Reach out to everyone following/starred the-agency-starter repo and retarget them to the-agency.
Discuss pulling captain into monitoring @AgencyGroupAI on x.com — premium account, may have API access. Explore what we can do with X API for community engagement and monitoring.

claude/dropbox/{principal}/{agent}/ — file staging area on master. dropbox push sends from worktree to master, dropbox fetch pulls from master to worktree. Handles all branch mechanics. Tied into ISCP dispatch notifications. Needs folder naming convention. Assigned to ISCP.
agency-init settings-template missing usr/** Read/Glob/Bash(ls) pre-approval — agents get permission-prompted reading their own directories
agency-init does not ship flag and handoff tools to target repos — Enforcement Triangle broken (skills exist without backing tools)
Need SessionStart validation hook: verify principal resolves, usr/ dir exists, required tools present, agency.yaml well-formed
MAR acronym not in CLAUDE-THEAGENCY.md — agents don't recognize it when principal uses it
Enforcement tooling for provenance headers: hookify rule to warn when code is written without What Problem / How & Why header. Telemetry to track compliance. Part of Enforcement Triangle.
Need /push skill — Enforcement Triangle for git push. Tool + Skill + Hookify. Prevents direct push to origin/main. Third violation this session. Must create branch, push branch, create PR via gh.
Enforcement Triangle gap for push: hookify warns but no skill provides the correct alternative. Tool + Skill + Hookify rule, all three.
SessionStart hook should mechanically force agent startup sequence — act on startup prose is unreliable. Need hook that reads agent registration and executes startup directives.
ISCP agent bootstrap: prompted for Read permission on own handoff and ls on own dispatches. Worktree settings need broader usr/** permissions.
Build own X/Twitter MCP server or tool integration, not third-party. Post articles/tweets, read mentions, read timeline, search. Needs X API Basic tier.
Jordan TODO: Set up X developer account at developer.x.com with @AgencyGroupAI. Need Basic tier for read access.

Create private repo the-agency-ai/the-agency-content for all content work: articles, book, workshops. Agency model. jordandm + jordan-of access. Jordan works it from both the-agency and monofolk contexts.

Start a 'Thoughts on Principles' document in content/ — capture emerging principles like 'tent poles that cover the area, no more no less' and 'progress over perfection' and 'iterate toward ideal, don't block on perfect'
Test result reporting service — database-backed, captures structured test results, wires into quality gates. Similar pattern to ISCP/dispatches. Discuss scope and relationship to ISCP.

ISCP agent getting prompted for git commit permission — git-commit tool needs pre-approved permissions in settings-template.json. Agents should not be approving commits on their own work. Feeds into Item 4 (pre-approved permissions).
